commit.message:
Fix initializer-safe bulk renaming and NameFixPass initializer iteration (#369)

## Summary

Fixes onnx/ir-py#353.

This PR addresses two related rename failures:

1. Direct rename loops fail for initializer-backed values in
swap/permutation cases.
2. `NameFixPass` can raise `RuntimeError: dictionary keys changed during
iteration` when a rename changes initializer dict keys while the pass is
iterating them.

## Changes

- add `onnx_ir.convenience.rename_values()` for bulk value renaming
- make initializer-backed multi-value renames safe by using temporary
names during swaps/permutations
- keep the existing initializer collision checks for names outside the
rename set
- make `NameFixPass` iterate over a snapshot of initializer values
instead of the live dict view
- add regression tests for:
  - swapping two initializer-backed outputs
- renaming to an existing initializer name before running `NameFixPass`

## Why

A single `value.name = ...` rename is not enough for safe initializer
swaps, because the initializer name guard correctly rejects taking
another live initializer name. The new bulk helper provides the missing
multi-rename operation, while the `NameFixPass` change makes renaming
safe when initializer dict keys are updated during the pass.

## Validation

- `pytest -q src/onnx_ir/_convenience/_convenience_test.py -k
rename_values`
- `pytest -q src/onnx_ir/passes/common/naming_test.py -k
initializer_collision`

Reported from downstream integration work in jax2onnx.

---------

Signed-off-by: enpasos <matthias.unverzagt@enpasos.com>
Co-authored-by: Copilot Autofix powered by AI <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: Justin Chu <justinchuby@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Developer tools (experimental): CLI and DevFlow documentation (#3277)

* Developer tools (experimental): CLI and DevFlow docs

Add new 'Developer tools (experimental)' section documenting the
.NET MAUI CLI and DevFlow toolkit from dotnet/maui-labs.

CLI docs (4 pages):
- Overview with installation, command reference, global options
- Environment diagnostics (maui doctor)
- Android SDK, JDK, and emulator management
- Device management

DevFlow docs (10 pages):
- Overview with architecture, quick start, packages, platform support
- Visual tree inspection and screenshots
- Element interaction and automation
- Blazor WebView debugging via CDP
- MCP server for AI agents (15 tool categories)
- Network monitoring and profiling
- Broker architecture (multi-app port assignment)
- Platform setup: Android, Apple, Windows

Every page includes a prominent experimental support disclaimer
noting these packages are not covered by the .NET MAUI Support Policy.

feat: filtered Bring upload from shopping list

Quando clicchi INVIA A BRING dalla lista della spesa:
1. Filtra SOLO ingredienti con checkbox spuntato
2. Se nessuno spuntato -> avviso
3. Se almeno 1 -> Li invia a Bring
4. RESETTA tutti gli ingredienti a non-spuntato
5. Salva nel DB

commit.message:
fix: remove 18 deprecated models reintroduced by PR #213

The scraper's deprecated filtering is coupled to --merge-regions,
which uses Azure's region API lifecycleStatus as the authoritative
source. PR #213 ran without --merge-regions, so deprecated models
were never filtered out.

Removed: dall-e-3, davinci-002, gpt-35-turbo, gpt-35-turbo-16k,
gpt-35-turbo-instruct, gpt-4, gpt-4-32k, gpt-4.5-preview,
gpt-4o-audio-preview, gpt-4o-mini-audio-preview,
gpt-4o-mini-realtime-preview, gpt-4o-realtime-preview, o1-mini,
o1-preview, sora, whisper, Kimi-K2-Thinking, MAI-DS-R1

commit.message:
refactor: agents use ask_user tool for clarification — no hardcoded menus

The LLM now drives ALL interactive disambiguation decisions.
Hardcoded Python input() menus and if-len>1 branching are replaced by
the ask_user tool, which the LLM calls only when genuine ambiguity exists.

tools/ask_user_tool.py (new)
- ask_user(question, options?) pauses the agent loop and collects user
  input; returns {answer, index} as a tool result back to the LLM
- TOOL_DEFINITION in Groq function-calling schema format
- Design note included: agents must never bypass this with raw input()

agents/exchange_disambiguator.py (refactored)
- Now a proper BaseAgent subclass with tools=[ASK_USER_TOOL]
- LLM system prompt instructs it to auto-select when obvious, and call
  ask_user when: multiple distinct companies OR multiple exchanges found
- Removed: _llm_auto_select, _resolve_single_company, _resolve_multiple_companies,
  _ask_exchange, _ask_to_refine (all replaced by the LLM tool loop)
- Kept: _group_by_company, _best_per_exchange, _find_group_for_ticker,
  _normalise_name (preprocessing helpers fed to the LLM as context)

orchestrator.py
- Removed _pick_company() static method (was hardcoded company menu)
- Removed Step B.5 company disambiguation call
- Removed _normalise_name import (no longer needed here)
- All candidate lists are passed to ExchangeDisambiguatorAgent as before;
  the agent's LLM loop handles both company AND exchange selection

.github/copilot-instructions.md
- Added Pattern 7: Agent Clarification Pattern (ask_user tool)
- Rule documented: no raw input() or hardcoded menus inside agents
- Updated Conventions: interactive prompts via ask_user only; only
  main.py may use raw input()
- Updated architecture diagram and file structure

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Implement comprehensive authentication system with Google Sign-In and onboarding

Features implemented:
- AuthViewModel with state management (AuthState sealed class)
- AuthenticationRepository centralizing Firebase operations
- Email/password auth with robust validation
- Google Sign-In integration with OAuth credentials
- OnboardingFragment for post-registration profile collection
- Password recovery functionality
- Session persistence with DataStore
- Comprehensive error handling with Italian user messages
- Material Design 3 UI with proper loading states
- Navigation graph updated with onboarding flow
- DI pattern using AuthViewModelProvider interface

Technical details:
- Uses Kotlin Coroutines and StateFlow for async operations
- Dual persistence: DataStore for preferences, Room for durable data
- ActivityResultContract for safe Google Sign-In
- Password strength validation (8+ chars, uppercase, number)
- Onboarding data validation (name, weight 30-300kg, height 100-250cm)
- All Firebase exceptions mapped to user-friendly messages

Files created:
- AuthViewModel.kt
- AuthenticationRepository.kt
- OnboardingFragment.kt
- fragment_onboarding.xml
- ic_google.xml
- edit_text_background.xml
- AUTHENTICATION_IMPLEMENTATION.md (comprehensive docs)

Files modified:
- AuthFragment.kt (complete rewrite with Google SignIn)
- MainActivity.kt (DI implementation)
- fragment_auth.xml (enhanced layout)
- nav_graph.xml (onboarding actions)
- build.gradle.kts (added Google Play Services Auth)
- strings.xml (40+ error/UI strings)

Note: Build requires Java 11, 17, or 21 (Java 26 has Kotlin compiler compatibility issues)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat(analysis): add pipeline chain inlay hints

Track which Call nodes in HIR were desugared from the |> pipeline
operator by recording their HirIds in a HashSet during lowering.
Thread the set through LowerResultMeta → TypedHir so that the inlay
hints module can identify them without changing the HIR structure.

For multi-line pipelines, emit a ChainedPipeline inlay hint at the end
of each LHS expression showing the intermediate type flowing into the
next step. Single-line pipelines are suppressed by comparing line
numbers in the existing span information.

- Add pipeline_call_ids: HashSet<HirId> to LowerResultMeta and
  LoweringContext; populate in lower_pipeline_operator
- Introduce HintCtx struct to bundle type_table, range, and
  pipeline_call_ids through hint collection helpers
- Add InlayHintKind::ChainedPipeline; wire up in LSP server and
  WASM bindings ("chainedPipeline" string kind)
- Add tests for multi-line and single-line pipeline hint behaviour

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat: ask user to select company when multiple distinct companies found

- Orchestrator._pick_company(): groups CompanySearchAgent results by
  normalised name so ADR/GDR/Old variants of the SAME company collapse
  into a single entry. Only truly distinct companies (e.g. 'Infosys
  Limited' vs 'Infosys BPM Limited') are shown as separate choices.
- Inserted Step B.5 in _resolve(): calls _pick_company() after Step B
  so the user picks the company BEFORE ticker searches run. This keeps
  the subsequent exchange menu focused on one company.
- Import _normalise_name from exchange_disambiguator into orchestrator
  so both use identical normalisation logic.
- _normalise_name: also strips parenthetical qualifiers (GDR), (Old),
  (ADR) so grouping is even more accurate.
- _find_group_for_ticker() added to ExchangeDisambiguatorAgent: after
  LLM auto-selects a ticker it looks up the company group; if that group
  has multiple exchange listings the exchange menu is shown instead of
  silently returning the LLM pick.

The homepage had two <h1> tags: one from the static SSR content injected
by staticPagesPlugin into div#root, and another in the <noscript> fallback.

the SSR H1 remains as the primary heading.

commit.message:
fix(ci): revert arguments DCE + remove dead code + add array push IC

- Revert CreateMappedArguments/CreateUnmappedArguments/CreateRestParameter
  DCE: removing these nodes from the graph changes register allocation
  and exposes latent regalloc bugs on Linux release builds (SIGSEGV,
  wrong results). They are now treated as side-effecting again. Codegen
  already handles them as cheap no-ops when they appear in the graph.

- Remove unused decode_jit_values function (clippy dead_code error).

- Add CallProp1IcEntry single-entry IC for arr.push() in CallProperty1
  dispatch. Caches callee handle to skip PropertyMap lookup on hot path.

Batch-translate all words via single AOAI call on text load

Replace per-word Azure Translator API calls with a single GPT-4o-mini
call that translates all unique words at once using full text context.

- translationService: new batchTranslateText() sends unique words +
  full text to GPT, returns Map<word, translation>
- ReadingSession: calls batchTranslateText on mount and language change,
  passes pre-computed map to WordPopup
- WordPopup: instant lookup from map (no API call, no loading state)
  Shows 'Translating...' only while batch call is in flight

Benefits: 1 API call instead of N, instant word popups, better context.

commit.message:
fix: mute immediately overridden by reconnect unmute sync

_sync_unmute_to_ma() fired on every sink_muted=False status update from
the daemon subprocess, not just after a (re)start. When mute was routed
through MA, the PA sink stayed unmuted, causing the sync to instantly
reverse the user's mute command.

Added _pending_reconnect_unmute_sync flag: set True on subprocess start,
consumed (set False) after the first unmute sync. Subsequent
sink_muted=False events no longer trigger MA unmute.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
perf(ObjectId): use InlineArray buffer with SIMD operations and vectorized hex conversion (#12978)

Replace the ulong/ulong/uint field representation with a [InlineArray(20)] fixed-size
byte buffer stored in big-endian SHA-1 natural order. This enables direct hex conversion
without endian swaps and SIMD-accelerated equality/comparison via SequenceEqual.

Storage:
- Replace three fields (ulong _i1, ulong _i2, uint _i3) with InlineArray Sha1 struct
- Data stored in big-endian byte order — hex conversion is a direct slice
- Sentinel values (WorkTreeId, IndexId, CombinedDiffId) now initialised via Parse

Parsing — Convert.FromHexString (SSSE3-vectorized):
- Replace ulong.TryParse/Utf8Parser with Convert.FromHexString for both char and byte
  overloads, which is SSSE3-vectorized in the .NET runtime
- TryParse(Span<char>):  36 ns → 18 ns  (2.0×)
- TryParse(Span<byte>):  46 ns → 17 ns  (2.7×)
- TryParse(string):      35 ns → 18 ns  (1.9×)

Validation — SearchValues (SIMD):
- IsValidCharacters already used SearchValues on upstream/master (retained)
- IsValid:               29 ns → 1.5 ns (19×)

ToString/ToShortString — string.Create + TryToHexStringLower:
- Even lengths: write hex directly into the string's char buffer via string.Create,
  single allocation, no intermediate ToHexStringLower + Substring
- ToString():            11 ns → 7 ns   (1.6×)
- ToShortString(8):      6 ns → 4 ns    (1.6×)

Equality — SequenceEqual (SIMD):
- Replace field-by-field comparison with ReadOnlySpan.SequenceEqual
- Equals and == benefit from SIMD 128-bit comparison

Comparison — SequenceCompareTo:
- Replace three-step field comparison with ReadOnlySpan.SequenceCompareTo
- CompareTo:             17 ns → 1.3 ns (13×), eliminates 24B boxing allocation

GetHashCode — Unsafe.ReadUnaligned:
- Read first 4 bytes directly instead of casting _i2
- SHA-1 has excellent entropy across all bytes

New members:
- WriteTo(Span<char>): zero-allocation hex write for callers that need the SHA-1
  in a buffer without allocating a string (e.g. ArgumentBuilder)
  - 5 ns / 0B vs ToString() at 31 ns / 104B  (6× faster)
- ISpanFormattable: enables zero-alloc string interpolation via TryFormat
- ArgumentBuilder.Add(ReadOnlySpan<char>): new overload to support span-based args
- ArgumentBuilderExtensions.Add(ObjectId): now uses WriteTo to avoid ToString alloc

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: skip agent queue creation if already exists in project

Uses azuredevops_agent_queues data source to check whether the pool is
already linked as a queue before attempting to create it. On re-runs
(or when the queue was created outside Terraform) this prevents the
'Agent pool already exists' error.

local.queue_already_exists drives the count on the resource.
local.queue_id resolves the ID from whichever source is active so
azuredevops_pipeline_authorization always gets a valid queue ID.

commit.message:
fix: catch git push exceptions for non-fast-forward retry

git push exits with non-zero on non-fast-forward rejection, causing
cli_execute_command to throw an exception rather than return output.
This bypassed the isPushFailure detection and retryAfterPushFailure
force-push retry. Now both the initial push and the force-push retry
are wrapped in try-catch so the error text is always captured and
the retry path is correctly triggered.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Fix(ci): parse manifest digest from text

The docker buildx imagetools inspect --format
template does not expose .Digest at the top
level (field not in tplInputs). Parse the
digest from default text output instead.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <claude@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
feat: add SentimentAgent with per-article sentiment and next-day price prediction

SentimentAgent (agents/sentiment_agent.py):
  - Takes ticker + curated news list from NewsAgent
  - Fetches last 5 trading days close prices from yfinance as price context
  - Single LLM call (llama-3.3-70b-versatile, temp 0.1) assigns:
      * per-article sentiment (positive/negative/neutral) + score 1-10
      * overall aggregate sentiment score
      * next-trading-day prediction (bullish/bearish/neutral)
      * confidence (high/medium/low) + estimated % move range + reasoning
  - Renders a markdown table with emoji indicators for display
  - Gracefully degrades to neutral-on-all on LLM failure

Orchestrator changes:
  - Imports and instantiates SentimentAgent alongside NewsAgent
  - New _fetch_sentiment() helper wraps the agent call safely
  - get_current_stock_price enrichment block appends sentiment report
  - get_company_info intent appends sentiment report
  - Disclaimer note included in output (not financial advice)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat: add dark/light theme toggle and PWA install banner

- ThemeToggle: cycles dark→light→system, persists to localStorage
- PwaInstallBanner: captures beforeinstallprompt, dismissable for 30 days
- Integrated ThemeToggle in DashboardLayout header bar
- Integrated PwaInstallBanner globally in App.tsx
- EmptyState component verified as working (no changes needed)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Co-authored-by: amarktainetwork2-collab <269954273+amarktainetwork2-collab@users.noreply.github.com>

feat: DAG layout uses happens-before ordering

Concurrent events appear on the same row in their peer columns.
Row = max(parent rows) + 1, so only causal dependencies push nodes
down. This makes the DAG more compact and visually clear.

Epic 6: frontend workout flow

- useWorkout.ts: composable with startWorkout, fetchWorkouts, fetchWorkout, addExercise, removeExercise, addSet, updateSet, deleteSet, completeWorkout; active workout ID persisted in localStorage

commit.message:
Add eval harness + fix article protection with leading punctuation

eval_mlm_augmentation.py:
- Visual regression test for MLM augmentation: entity preservation,
  stray-mask check, context window diffs, prose sentence diffs
- Selects stories automatically (PERSON + LOCATION + AGENT present)
  or via --story-ids for pinned regression targets
- Highlights changed words inline (red=removed green=added)
- Shows ±5-word context windows around each entity before/after
- Fixed change-rate metric to use difflib SequenceMatcher instead of
  zip-alignment, which drifted badly on long stories (was reporting
  84% change rate on unchanged paragraphs)

augment_mlm_context.py:
- Fix article protection to strip leading punctuation (quotes, em-dash)
  before checking if token is 'the/a/an'.  '"The Metropolitan...' was
  not being protected because '"the'.rstrip('.,') != 'the'.
  Using str.maketrans to strip all common quote/punct variants.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
